make the following sentence better by any means, creating new sentences is fine, fix grammar, and you can also replace words as you wish. Basically, make it fluid and easy to read.

Text:Along with education, I've also eagerly taken opportunities to volunteer for the sake of the environment, start a club for free music education to kids in need, and respect and salute our elderly by performing various pieces and concertos at our local retirement home.

In addition to my education, I have enthusiastically embraced opportunities to contribute to environmental causes through volunteering. I also took the initiative to establish a club aimed at providing free music education to underprivileged children. Moreover, I express my deep appreciation for senior citizens by performing a wide range of musical pieces and concertos at our community's retirement home, showcasing my respect and admiration for them.

Text:Along with education, I’ve also eagerly taken opportunities to volunteer for the sake of the environment, start a club for free music education to kids in need, and respect and salute our elderly by performing various pieces and concertos at our local retirement home.

In addition to pursuing my education, I have actively sought out opportunities to volunteer and make a positive impact on the environment. Furthermore, I seized the chance to establish a club dedicated to providing free music education to children in need. Additionally, I express my utmost respect and admiration for our elderly population by performing a diverse selection of pieces and concertos at our nearby retirement home, aiming to bring joy and appreciation to their lives.

write the best previous sentence to the text below. keep in mind that the sentence you provide should play into the same themes as the provided text, and how it talks about how we want to give music lessons to passionate middle schoolers who can't afford lessons.

Provided text:With these thoughts in mind, I decided to create a school program where high schoolers would provide free lessons to middle schoolers once a week. Backed by the SKJAJA fund, Young leaders in music now has over 50 high schoolers and almost 40 middle schoolers who are excited to teach and learn music.

Driven by these same values, I took the initiative to develop a program that supports and nurtures the musical talents of passionate middle schoolers who may not have access to formal lessons. Inspired by the SKJAJA fund, our organization, Young Leaders in Music, has grown to include more than 50 enthusiastic high school students and nearly 40 eager middle school students, all eager to teach and learn music together on a weekly basis.

In a world where the rhythm of life often echoes disparities, we refuse to let financial barriers silence the untapped melodies that resonate within passionate middle schoolers. Thus, driven by a fervent belief in the transformative power of music, a visionary school program took shape—an invaluable opportunity for high schoolers to compassionately share their knowledge and talent with deserving middle school students, uniting hearts and harmonies once a week. Backed by the SKJAJA fund, the Young Leaders in Music initiative now thrives, enlisting over 50 dedicated high school mentors who eagerly join forces with almost 40 enthusiastic middle school prodigies, as they embark on a harmonious journey of teaching and learning, creating a symphony of hope and growth.

Provided text:With these thoughts in mind, I decided to create a school program where high schoolers would provide free lessons to middle schoolers once a week. Backed by the SKJAJA fund, Young leaders in music now has over 50 high schoolers and almost 40 middle schoolers who are excited to teach and learn music.

Amidst the echoes of missed opportunities and unfulfilled dreams, we have forged a path to rewrite the narrative for passionate middle schoolers, whose yearning for music education has remained unmet due to financial constraints. Driven by this shared plight, a visionary undertaking was born, weaving together the threads of compassion and dedication. It is in this backdrop that a transformative school program emerged, where empathetic high school students generously offer their expertise, providing free weekly music lessons to ignite the flames of creativity and empower these talented young souls. Today, under the benevolent wings of the SKJAJA fund, the Young Leaders in Music initiative has gathered a fervent family of over 50 high school mentors, united in purpose, resonating with the excitement of nearly 40 middle school protégés, as they embark on a harmonious journey of teaching, learning, and limitless possibilities.

Provided text:With these thoughts in mind, I decided to create a school program where high schoolers would provide free lessons to middle schoolers once a week. Backed by the SKJAJA fund, Young leaders in music now has over 50 high schoolers and almost 40 middle schoolers who are excited to teach and learn music.

As I reflect upon my own journey, where the symphony of music became my solace amidst the struggles of my youth, my heart aches for those impassioned middle schoolers whose dreams dance to the same rhythm, yet are shackled by financial limitations. Inspired by this empathy, a fervent desire to rewrite their narrative took hold, propelling me to create a groundbreaking school program. In this transformative endeavor, high schoolers selflessly extend their hands, offering a harmonious gift of free music lessons, once a week, to kindle the flames of possibility and illuminate the path to their dreams. With the unwavering support of the SKJAJA fund, the Young Leaders in Music initiative now stands tall, counting more than 50 determined high school mentors and close to 40 eager middle schoolers, forming a resounding chorus united by the pure joy of teaching and learning.

make this sentence more fluid and heartfelt

Sentence:I am truly thankful of the opportunitiy and the privilege I had growing up, being able to afford private lessons for clarinet, but I realized that young, talented individuals could be wasting their talent because of rediculously high lesson fees.

Grateful for the opportunities and privileges that blessed my own upbringing, where the melodies of the clarinet echoed through transformative private lessons, my heart ached at the realization that countless young souls, brimming with immense talent, remain shackled by exorbitant lesson fees that threaten to suffocate their dreams.

make this sentence more fluid 

Sentence:I am truly thankful of the opportunitiy and the privilege I had growing up, being able to afford private lessons for clarinet, but I realized that young, talented individuals could be wasting their talent because of rediculously high lesson fees.

With deep gratitude for the immense opportunity and privilege I enjoyed during my upbringing, as I had the means to afford private clarinet lessons, a poignant realization struck me: the unfortunate reality that young, exceptionally gifted individuals may never have the chance to nurture and share their talents due to exorbitant lesson fees that mark an unjust barrier to their dreams.
